---
engine: knitr
---
# Krona
<center>
![](/images/krona.png){style="width:150px; background: white; border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
</center>

`Krona` is an interactive metagenome species abundance visualisation tool.
We will use it to produce interactive tiered pie charts from our `Kraken2` output.

## Kraken2 report to krona

Prior to creating our html pie charts we need to convert our kraken2 reports to krona text files.
We'll carry this out with the [`KrakenTools`](https://github.com/jenniferlu717/KrakenTools) script: `kreport2krona.py`.

::: {.callout-note}
Ensure you type the full python script as it is below. <br>
For information on how to download and use the python script in your own computer/environment please see the [appendix](/appendix/mamba/qmd#shotgun_meta)
:::

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#Krona conversion for K1
python ~/git_installs/KrakenTools/kreport2krona.py -r K1.kreport2 -o K1.krona
#Krona conversion for K2
python ~/git_installs/KrakenTools/kreport2krona.py -r K2.kreport2 -o K2.krona
#Krona conversion for W1
python ~/git_installs/KrakenTools/kreport2krona.py -r W1.kreport2 -o W1.krona
```

- `-r` is our input report file
- `-o` is our output krona text file

`r hide("Loop method")`
Rather than running the command for each line you can carry it out with a loop.
If you run the below first ensure your current directory only has `.kreport2` files that you want included.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
ls -1 *kreport2 | sed "s/.kreport2//" | while read sample
do 
python /pub14/tea/nsc206/git_installs/KrakenTools/kreport2krona.py \
-r ${sample}.kreport2 \
-o ${sample}.krona
done
```
`r unhide()`

The actual content of the krona text file is not of interest as the file is only really useful for creating the interactive krona charts.

## Import taxonomy
<center>
![](/images/play_red.png){style="width:100px; background: white; border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
</center>

Time to make our `krona` charts. 
With the below command we can import our `krona` text files into one `Krona` html.

```{bash eval=FALSE}
ktImportText -o kraken2.krona.html *.krona
```

- `-o` is our output html file
- The final argument `*.krona` represents all of our `.krona` files in the current directory
  - The `*` is a wild-card, meaning any characters any number of times. Therefore `*.krona` identifies the files `K1.krona` `K2.krona` and `W1.krona`

## Tiered piecharts
<center>
![](/images/eye_red.png){style="width:100px; background: white; border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
</center>

We can view our interactive chart in a web browser. This shows the percentage of reads that were classified to various taxonomies at different levels.

```{bash eval=FALSE}
firefox kraken2.krona.html &
```

This is an interactive multi-tiered pie chart with many options. Some of the interactivity is described below:

- To choose a sample to view, click on the sample name in the top left
- To zoom into a lower taxonomy, double click on the taxonomy's name on the pie chart
  - E.g. double click the word "Bacteria" on the pie chart to zoom into Bacteria and therefore ignore the Unclassified, Viral, and Archaeal
- To zoom out to a higher taxonomy, click on the taxonomy's name in the middle of the pie chart
  - E.g. click the word "all" in the middle of the pie chart to zoom back out from any lower level of taxonomy
- To view percentage stats of a taxonomy, click on the taxonomy name on the pie chart.
  - This will display the percentage this taxonomy covers of all the different taxonomies above it that it belongs to. This is displayed on the right side of the screen
  - E.g. Click on Pseudomonadota to see the percentage of all the taxa it accounts for and to see the percentage of Bacteria it accounts for in the sample
- With our method of kraken2 to krona the top right of the screen shows the following 2 metrics for the slected taxa:
  - __Total:__ This is the "Number of paired reads covered by the clade rooted at this taxon" (i.e. column 2 of the kreport file)
  - __Unassigned:__ This is appears to be the "Number of paired reads assigned directly to this taxon". However, the number for "k_Bacteria" does not seem to match anything in the `.kreport2` files

## MCQs
<center>
![](/images/question_bubble_red.png){style="width:100px; background: white; border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
</center>

Viewing the Krona, attempt the below MCQs.

```{r, echo = FALSE}
opts_p <- c("__34%__", answer="__44%__", "__67%__")
```
1. What percentage of __all__ was classified to __Bacteria__ for __K1__? `r longmcq(opts_p)`

```{r, echo = FALSE}
opts_p <- c(answer="__14%__", "__44%__","__67%__")
```
2. What percentage of __bacteria__ was classified to __Firmicutes__ (also known as Bacillota) for __K2__? `r longmcq(opts_p)`

```{r, echo = FALSE}
opts_p <- c("__14%__", "__44%__", answer="__67%__")
```
3. What percentage of the __bacteroidetes__ was classified to __Bacteroides__ for __W1__? `r longmcq(opts_p)`

```{r, echo = FALSE}
opts_p <- c("**15**",answer="**17,244**","**105,847**")
```
4. What is the number of paired reads covered by the clade rooted at _Anaerostipes hadrus_ (i.e. Total) for K2? `r longmcq(opts_p)`

```{r, echo = FALSE}
opts_p <- c("**15**","**17,244**",answer="**105,847**")
```
5. What is the number of paired reads covered by the clade rooted at _Bacteroides ovatus_ (i.e. Total) for __K1__? `r longmcq(opts_p)`

```{r, echo = FALSE}
opts_p <- c(answer="**15**","**17,244**","**105,847**")
```
6. What is the number of paired reads assigned directly to __Clostridia__ (i.e. Unassigned) for __W1__?  `r longmcq(opts_p)`
